panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Cultural Properties Reports - Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute] Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Cultural Properties Reports - Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute] The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.

Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. === Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 5]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Cultural Properties Reports - Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute] These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * === Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Cultural Properties Reports - Nara Cultural Properties Research Institute] === Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * {{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Information from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various forms, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. Built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. Built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). Artifacts such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. Built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. Built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
