Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.

These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション=二股古墳 |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4世紀～7世紀 |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |有形文化財= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=操山古墳群 |別名= |画像=[[ファイル:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション=二股古墳 |所在地=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |築造時期=4世紀～7世紀 |史跡指定=未指定 |有形文化財= |規模=130基 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=操山古墳群|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }}
'''操山古墳群'''（みさおやまこふんぐん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]の[[操山]]丘陵一帯にある[[古墳群]]である。
== 概要 == 標高169メートルの操山から円山、笠井山に連なる丘陵地に約130基の古墳が確認されている。4世紀～5世紀の[[古墳時代]]前期から中期の古墳が約30基、6世紀～7世紀の古墳時代後期から終末期の古墳が約100基となっている。丘陵東部の笠井山には約30基の古墳があり特に笠井山古墳支群と呼ばれる<ref>岡山市設置現地説明板より</ref>。
==主な古墳== 操山古墳群の古墳の形体は、[[前方後円墳]]、[[円墳]]、[[方墳]]など様々である。後期から終末期にかけての古墳は合葬が可能な石組みの[[横穴式石室]]を持ち、丘陵全体に散在している。確認されている古墳には、番号が割り当てられている。ここでは特に名称のついている古墳を列記する。
===萩の塚古墳=== '''萩の塚古墳'''（はぎのづかこふん）は、操山山頂から東に延びた稜線の先端に位置する。操山9号墳。円墳で、墳丘は落剥により小振りなものとなっているが、本来は直径10メートル・高さ2.5メートル程度と推定されている。6世紀後半の造営と思われる。石室は横穴式で長さ8.2メートル・幅1.9メートル・高さ1.9メートルである。操山北麓の有力者の家族墓と推定される。近年まで石室内には防火用水・用具が置かれており、天井石には”火の用心”のペンキ跡が残っている。
===旗振台古墳=== '''旗振台古墳'''（はたふりだいこふん）は、操山の南西部、古代の瀬戸内海航路を望む尾根頂部に位置する。操山14号墳。[[明治時代]]中頃、大阪で米相場が立つと旗を振って知らせる連絡所がこの近くにあったことに由来する<ref>[http://townweb.e-okayamacity.jp/tomiyama-r/ 富山学区連合町内会HP]</ref>。一辺27メートル、高さ4メートルの方墳である。築造時期は古墳時代前期の5世紀前半と推定されている。墳頂部には葺石が施され、[[形象埴輪]]・[[円筒埴輪]]が巡らされていた。墳頂部中央に[[竪穴式石室]]が認められる。石室両脇に[[粘土槨]]がある。石室からは短甲・剣・刀・鉄鏃など、粘土槨からは冑・短甲・剣・刀・鉄鏃・ガラス玉・瑪瑙玉・勾玉などが出土した。
===八畳岩古墳=== '''八畳岩古墳'''（はちじょういわこふん）は、操山山頂と金蔵山古墳の中間付近の操山山塊主稜線上に位置する。操山19号墳。築造時期は古墳時代後期の6世紀後半と推定されている。直径15メートル、高さ3メートル程度の円墳であったと推計されているが墳丘は流失し概ね失われ横穴式石室が剥き出しとなっている。露出した石室の大きさが呼び名となった。石室は全長9メートル、玄室は長さ5.5メートル・幅1.6メートル・高さ1.6メートル。操山丘陵の北麓に住んでいた有力者の家族墓と思われる。
===二股古墳=== '''二股古墳'''（ふたまたこふん）は、八畳岩古墳と金蔵山古墳の中間付近の斜面に位置する。操山24号墳。斜面に築造された山寄せ造りの円墳で直径10メートル、高さ3メートルであったと推計される。6世紀末から7世紀初頭の古墳時代後期に造営されたと推定されている。横穴式の石室が二股に分かれた珍しい構造をしており、名称の由来となっている。開口部向かって左側が主室で長さ5メートル・幅1.2メートル・高さ1.7メートル、右側の脇室は長さ4.5メートル・幅1.2メートル・高さ1.3メートル。
===金蔵山古墳=== {{see|[[金蔵山古墳]]}} '''金蔵山古墳'''（かなくらやまこふん）は、操山丘陵のほぼ中央に位置し、標高115メートルの山頂にある前方後円墳である。操山30号墳。4世紀末から5世紀初頭に造営されたと推定されている。全長165メートル、後円部は径110メートル・高さ18メートル。岡山県下4番目の規模である。前方部が北方向に向いている。くびれ部には[[造り出し]]が認められる。後円部3段、前方部2段の造りで埴輪が巡らされ、斜面には[[葺石]]が葺かれる。
[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に後円部の発掘調査が行われた。後円部頂中央とその南側に[[竪穴式石槨]]が確認された。中央部石槨の方が古い時代に造られている。石槨周辺から[[埴輪]]も家形、楯形など多数出土した。中央石槨は概ね盗掘されていたが、筒型銅器、鉄製[[武具]]が発見された。中央石槨には副室があり、盗掘を免れており[[鑿]]・[[錐 (工具)|錐]]・[[鋸]]などの工具を主とした鉄器が発掘された。南側石槨も盗掘に遭っているが、国産の変形二神四獣鏡、[[玉]]、鉄製武具などが出土した。[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）の発掘調査では後円部法面の調査が行われ、円筒埴輪・形象埴輪が出土するとともに、墳端埴輪列と葺石の確認作業がなされた。また、[[2014年]]度（平成26年度）の発掘調査では造出付近において大首長級古墳に見られる柵形埴輪列が、[[2015年]]度（平成27年度）の調査では造出部分において祭祀を行うためと見られる石敷遺構が<ref>[http://www.sanyonews.jp/article/293875 "岡山・金蔵山古墳に石敷き遺構 造り出しで出土、祭祀空間演出か"]（山陽新聞、2016年2月2日記事）。</ref>、[[2016年]]度（平成28年度）の調査では[[湊茶臼山古墳]]にも見られる島状遺構が発見されている<ref>[http://www.sanyonews.jp/article/488578 "岡山・金蔵山古墳で島状遺構出土 祭祀場か、25日に現地説明会"]（山陽新聞、2017年2月16日記事）。</ref>。
===沢田大塚古墳=== {{see|[[沢田大塚古墳]]}} '''沢田大塚古墳'''（さわだおおつかこふん）は、円山の北西尾根山麓付近、沢田集落の近くに位置する。操山38号墳。円墳で直径16メートル・高さ4.7メートル。古墳時代後期の造営。石室は横穴式で全長11.4メートル、玄室は長さ4.8メートル・幅2.4メートル・高さ3.2メートル、羨道は長さ6.4メートル・幅1.7メートル。大人が十分に立てる規模の大きな石室が築かれている。
=== その他 === 護国神社裏山古墳（13号墳）、柿の木古墳（33号墳、円墳）、石鉄山（いしぐろやま）古墳（54号墳、円墳、円山山頂）、経𡉕（きょうぐろ）古墳（96号墳、円墳）などがある。
== 画像 == <gallery> ファイル:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|萩の塚古墳 ファイル:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|旗振台古墳 ファイル:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|八畳岩古墳 ファイル:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|金蔵山古墳 ファイル:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|沢田大塚古墳 ファイル:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|石鉄山古墳 </gallery>
==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 2000年 58-61ページ *岡山市教育委員会文化財課 岡山市埋蔵文化財センター/編 『[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/4402 金蔵山古墳-後円部墳端の調査-]』 岡山市教育委員/発行 2008年 *現地説明板 岡山市・岡山森林管理署/設置 *
== 注釈 == <references />
==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja 全国遺跡報告総覧 奈良文化財研究所]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みさおやまこふんくん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{citation needed|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{citation needed|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Ward, Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama City|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
