This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Naka Ward, Okayama|ja|中区 (岡山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Ward, Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama City|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]]

Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[en:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]]

=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.

Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[:en:岡山県|岡山県]][[:en:岡山市|岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Many kofun from the late to final Kofun period feature stone-built {{ill|yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal burial chambers) suitable for collective burial. Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.
=== Futamata Kofun === '''Futamata Kofun''' (二股古墳) lies on a slope near Hachijoiwa and Kanakurayama Kofun. Known as Misaoyama No. 24, it is a circular kofun measuring 10 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. built in the late 6th to early 7th century, it features a unique two-branch horizontal stone chamber, with the main chamber measuring 5 meters in length.
=== Kanakurayama Kofun === {{main|Kanakurayama Kofun}} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a large keyhole-shaped kofun located at the center of the Misaoyama Hills. Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement. Known as Misaoyama No. 38, it has a diameter of 16 meters and a height of 4.7 meters. built in the late Kofun period, its horizontal stone chamber measures 11.4 meters in total length.
== images == File:Haginotsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Haginotsuka Kofun File:Hatafuridai Kofun 01.JPG|Hatafuridai Kofun File:Hachijoiwa Kofun 04.JPG|Hachijoiwa Kofun File:Kanakurayama Kofun panorama.jpg|Kanakurayama Kofun File:Sawada Otsuka Kofun 02.JPG|Sawada Otsuka Kofun File:Ishiguroyama Kofun 01.JPG|Ishiguroyama Kofun == References == * Norioka Minoru, Gyoda Hiromi / Editors; Kondo Yoshio / Editorial Assistance, ''Kibi no Kofun: Bizen and Mimasaka'' (Kibi Archaeology Library 4), Kibijin Publishing, 2000, pp. 58-61. * Okayama City Board of Education, ''Kanakurayama Kofun: Investigation of the Rear Mound'' (2008). * On-site panels installed by Okayama City and Okayama Forest Management Office. == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Misaoyama Kofun Group}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] == External Links == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja National Repository of Historic Site Reports - Nara Historic Site Research Institute] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|操山古墳群|version=92887973|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Misaoyama Kofun Group]] [[ja:操山古墳群]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Misaoyama Kofun Group}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11498771]] Wikidata name: Misaoyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |native_name=操山古墳群 |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Futamata Kofun 03.JPG|250px]] |location=[[:en:岡山県|岡山県]][[:en:岡山市|岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]] |coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=4th century to 7th century |Historic Site Designation=未指定 |coordinates= |area=130基 |Special Notes= |coordinates = {{location map|Japan Okayama|map_dot_label=操山古墳群|coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=39.04|lon_deg=133|lon_min=57|lon_sec=19.89|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=岡山県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|39.04|N|133|57|19.89|E}}}} }} '''Misaoyama Kofun Group''' (操山古墳群) is a cluster of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in [[:en:Naka-ku, Okayama|Naka Ward, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama|Okayama City]], Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Approximately 130 kofun have been identified across the Misaoyama Hills, extending from Misaoyama (elevation 169 meters) through Maruyama and Kasaiyama. These kofun date from the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] (4th–5th centuries) and the late to final Kofun period (6th–7th centuries). Around 30 kofun belong to the earlier period, while about 100 are from the latter period. The eastern Kasaiyama area includes a subgroup of approximately 30 kofun, known collectively as the {{ill|Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|笠井山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaiyama Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Intypeation from on-site panels installed by Okayama City. == Major Kofun == The kofun in the Misaoyama Kofun Group exhibit various types, including [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds), {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mounds), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds). Below are notable kofun in this group. === Haginotsuka Kofun === '''Haginotsuka Kofun''' (萩の塚古墳) is located on the ridge extending eastward from the summit of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 9, it is a small circular kofun with an estimated original diameter of 10 meters and height of 2.5 meters. built in the late 6th century, it features a horizontal stone chamber measuring 8.2 meters in length, 1.9 meters in width, and 1.9 meters in height. It is believed to have served as a family tomb for a prominent household in northern Misaoyama. Remnants of "fire prevention" signage remain visible on the chamber ceiling, a relic of its modern usage.
=== Hatafuridai Kofun === '''Hatafuridai Kofun''' (旗振台古墳) is situated on a ridge in the southwestern part of Misaoyama, offering views of the ancient Seto Inland Sea route. Known as Misaoyama No. 14, it is a square kofun with a side length of 27 meters and a height of 4 meters. built in the early 5th century, the mound is covered with stones and surrounded by rows of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] (clay figurines). burial goods such as iron armor, swords, and beads have been unearthed from the burial chamber.
=== Hachijoiwa Kofun === '''Hachijoiwa Kofun''' (八畳岩古墳) is located along the central ridge of Misaoyama. Known as Misaoyama No. 19, this 6th-century circular kofun originally measured 15 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height. The mound has eroded, exposing its horizontal burial chamber, which measures 9 meters in total length. This kofun likely served as a family tomb for local elites.

Known as Misaoyama No. 30, it measures 165 meters in length, with a 110-meter-wide circular rear mound reaching a height of 18 meters. This kofun dates to the late 4th or early 5th century and is notable for its extensive haniwa decorations and burial chambers. === Sawada Otsuka Kofun === {{main|Sawada Otsuka Kofun}} '''Sawada Otsuka Kofun''' (沢田大塚古墳) is a circular kofun located near the Sawada settlement.
